rationale: Gate-integrity attack. (a) Threshold-expression path is SOUND: R7 falls back to N-1 for negatives, oversized, float, whitespace, and any garbage, so nothing unparseable can weaken; only in-range [1,N] integers pass, and the floor at 1 (not 0) is the correct always-pass defense. (b) GAP: R5 bounds the threshold integer but NOT the reviewers list. reviewers:[{claude,1}] (sum=1) with default "n-1" passes R5, and 110 would stamp a panel where ApproveThreshold("n-1",N=1)=0 -- a VALID expression, so R7's integer-fallback never fires. The spec's 'closed both sides' claim is false here; only the unmentioned gate.go:257 `threshold>0` guard saves it (fail-CLOSED today, but an always-pass if a future editor removes that guard trusting R5/R7). (c) The ADR-0037 amendment overstates: SS6/8 ARE unchanged, but S3 is EXTENDED (a recorded in-range integer overrides the N-1 default), not 'semantically unchanged'. (d) gate_authority is surfaced by `config show` with no inert marker; a who-passes-a-gate knob reading as active is a footgun. All paths fail closed today, so REQUEST_CHANGES, not a hard block.
concrete_changes_required: R5: add a config-side reviewer floor -- each reviewers[].count >= 1 AND sum(reviewers.count) >= 2 -- refused at Load with a recovery line, so a reviewers list can never silently derive an ApproveThreshold of <= 0 that only the unmentioned gate.go:257 `threshold>0` guard catches. This is the config-side analogue of R7's [1,N] record-side floor and is what actually makes the 'threshold-0 loophole closed both sides / defense in depth' claim (BRIEF + R5) true for the reviewers-count derivation, not just for a literal approve_threshold:0.

Name the existing gate.go:257 `&& threshold > 0` guard as a load-bearing THIRD defense (record-side R7 + config-side R5 + gate-side guard) in R7/R8, and pin it with an assertion in TestApproveThreshold_InterpretsRecordedExpr / TestPanelGateDecision_ConfigDefaultDoesNotAlterDecision, so a future editor cannot remove it believing R5/R7 make a 0 threshold unreachable -- the reviewers:[{fam,1}] -> "n-1" -> 0 path proves it IS reachable.

Fix the ADR-0037 amendment wording (R7 / ADR Touchpoints / Non-Goals): state that S3's threshold rule is EXTENDED -- an optional recorded in-range integer overrides the N-1 default, still single-homed in ApproveThreshold(), N-1 when the field is absent -- rather than 'semantically unchanged'. Keep the accurate claims that SS6 and 8 are unchanged and that an absent field is byte-identical N-1.

R4 (and R9's config-show rendering): state explicitly that loop.gate_authority -- and the rest of loop:/models:/runner: -- is INERT until a spec-110+ consumer enforces it; every approve gate stays human-invoked today regardless of the configured value. Either say this in R4 and/or annotate the surfaced blocks in `config show` as 'declared, not yet enforced', so a surfaced `spec_approve: panel` cannot be mistaken for an active authority delegation.
findings: (a) threshold-expression bounds/fallback: SOUND. Negatives, oversized (> N), floats, whitespace, and arbitrary garbage all fall back to N-1 (strict); only in-range [1,N] integers pass through; the floor at 1 (not 0) is the correct de-facto-panel-skip defense. Whitespace-trim behavior is left unspecified but is safe either way (it only affects whether a legitimately-padded value is honored, never whether the gate weakens).

(b) reviewers-list floor: GAP (change 1). reviewers:[{fam,1}] -> sum 1 -> default "n-1" -> ApproveThreshold = 0, un-refused config-side (R5 only bounds integers) and NOT caught record-side ("n-1" is a valid expr, so R7's fallback never runs). Only gate.go:257 `threshold>0` prevents an always-pass (fail-closed today). Same class: count <= 0, sum 0, and negative counts are all un-validated by R2/R5.

(c) ADR-0037 amendment: SS6 (fail-open/fail-closed asymmetry) and S8 (anti-footgun trust boundary) ARE genuinely unchanged -- the new agent-writable approve_threshold field fits S8's model and R7's out-of-range->N-1 fallback IS the anti-footgun posture. But S3 is EXTENDED, not unchanged: its universal 'threshold is N-1' now has a recorded-integer override. Single-home invariant preserved; wording must reflect the extension (change 3).

(d) gate_authority declaration: honest that enforcement is deferred (Goal/R4/Scope all say so), but `config show` surfaces the value with no inert marker, and for a WHO-passes-a-gate knob an inert-but-official-looking declaration risks over-trust (operator skips their own review) or confusion. One disclosing sentence closes it (change 4).

Confirmed good: PanelGateDecision stays config-free/pure and takes no config input (R8); internal/panel remains a dependency-clean leaf (the go list -deps proof is in Validation Proofs); zero-config round-trip and byte-identical absent-field N-1 are preserved; ReviewerCountNote is advisory-only and never flips Allow/Block; ADR-0040 Accepted-on-landing follows the 0034/0037 codification-ADR precedent soundly.
